title: Bind merge dispatch to a fresh atomic lease snapshot
goal: Close the race between an earlier repository review and the irreversible GitHub merge effect by atomically re-reading overlapping leases immediately before merge dispatch and binding that fresh snapshot to the exact pull request head, base and complete diff.

approval.note: Implement and test the merge-control gate only. It grants no merge authority and may never release or override a foreign lease.

acceptance.assertion: Immediately before merge dispatch, the control plane performs an authoritative uncached read of repository, branch, path, service, deployment and merge-gate leases relevant to the exact pull request effect.

The final lease read and merge intent are serialized under a short atomic critical section or equivalent compare-and-swap boundary so that a newly acquired overlapping lease cannot enter unnoticed between validation and dispatch.

The merge receipt binds repository, pull request number, base SHA, head SHA, complete GitHub diff SHA-256, relevant resource-key set, lease snapshot digest and comparable observation and dispatch timestamps.
Any foreign overlapping lease acquired after an earlier review or diff delivery invalidates the merge attempt and fails closed; cached evidence cannot be reused.
The gate never releases, expires, reconciles or force-overrides a foreign lease and never interprets an unscoped legacy lease as disjoint without an authoritative non-conflict proof.

If GitHub reports an external merge, the system records the observed lease state and whether its own pre-merge contract was satisfied, but performs no duplicate merge and does not retroactively manufacture compliance.

Tests cover a lease acquired between review and merge, stale lease snapshots, exact non-overlap, legacy unscoped leases, concurrent merge actors, external merge reconciliation and the successful atomic path.
A fresh lease snapshot is necessary but insufficient for merge: authorization, current diff delivery, critical review, green CI, recovery and branch protection remain independent required gates.

metadata.observation: A foreign repo-wide lease was acquired after the initial live-state inspection and before this actor dispatched the merge. The lease was discovered during runtime synchronization. The reviewed head, diff, tests and merge tree remained valid, but pre-merge lease freshness was not serialized.

relationship_to_existing.GRABOWSKI-OPERATOR-SURFACE-V1-T048: T048 serializes user-visible diff delivery before merge; T057 separately serializes the final lease state immediately before the merge effect.

metadata.risk: A cooperating actor can pass an early lease check and later merge after another owner has acquired an overlapping resource, producing valid code but invalid operator ordering evidence.
does_not_establish: that every external GitHub actor can be locked
future merge safety until T057 is implemented
